evals.name: Simulate model with observable referencing constant parameter
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly handles observables that reference constant parameters — a common source of NaN results when StatesToLog is misconfigured.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Simulate for 24 hours and show me Drug and DrugConc values at t=0, 12, and 24. I want to confirm the concentration relationship holds at each timepoint.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
DrugConc values are not NaN — constant parameter was properly logged
assertions.expected: DrugConc values are numeric (not NaN) at t=0, 12, 24

assertions.description: DrugConc = Drug / Vd relationship holds at displayed timepoints
assertions.expected: DrugConc values match Drug / 10 at each timepoint

evals.name: Compare dose levels across clearance values
evals.description: Tests whether the agent can set up a factorial parameter/dose comparison efficiently — requires correct use of scenarios or equivalent approach.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. I want to compare two dose levels (100mg and 400mg bolus) across three elimination rates (ke = 0.05, 0.1, 0.2). That's 6 combinations. Simulate all of them for 48 hours and show me the Drug amount at t=48 for each combination. I want to confirm that higher clearance leads to less drug remaining.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect Scenarios API usage
All 6 combinations are simulated and reported
assertions.expected: 6 distinct simulation results shown

assertions.description: Higher ke produces lower Drug at t=48 within each dose group
assertions.expected: Drug(t=48) decreases with increasing ke for both dose levels

evals.name: Stochastic simulation of gene expression
evals.description: Tests whether the agent configures the stochastic solver correctly — SSA has strict requirements on reaction kinetics that differ from ODE simulation.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a simple gene expression model: one gene produces mRNA (transcription rate 0.1), mRNA produces protein (translation rate 0.5), mRNA degrades (rate 0.05), protein degrades (rate 0.01). Gene is present at 1 copy. Run 50 stochastic simulations for 200 time units and report the mean and standard deviation of protein level at the end. I want to see the variability.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches custom rate expressions that are incompatible with stochastic solvers
Mean and standard deviation of protein at t=200 are reported from 50 runs
assertions.expected: Mean and std reported; std > 0 confirming stochastic variability

evals.name: Identify most influential parameters via sensitivity analysis
evals.description: Tests whether the agent can run global sensitivity analysis with correct function signatures and interpret the results.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/TwoCompPK.sbproj. Which parameters have the most influence on Drug concentration over 24 hours? Use ranges: ke (0.01-1), ka (0.1-5), k12 (0.01-1), k21 (0.01-1). Run a global sensitivity analysis and rank them for me.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect function signatures for sensitivity tools
Sensitivity indices are reported for all 4 parameters
assertions.expected: ke

assertions.description: ka parameter included in sensitivity results
assertions.expected: ka

assertions.description: k12 parameter included in sensitivity results
k21 parameter included in sensitivity results
Parameters are ranked by influence with interpretation
assertions.expected: Clear ranking from most to least influential parameter based on sensitivity indices

evals.name: Efficient parameter sweep across elimination rates
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses an efficient approach for repeated simulations with varying parameters instead of recompiling on each iteration.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. I want to see how elimination rate affects this model. Compare ke values of 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, and 0.5 over 24 hours. Show me the Drug amount remaining at t=24 for each value.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect createSimFunction argument patterns
4 Drug values at t=24 are produced and monotonically decrease with ke

evals.name: Correctly interpret factorial scenario results
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly maps factorial scenario results to their conditions. Results are interleaved by the first dimension — misinterpretation leads to silently swapped labels with no error.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. I want a factorial comparison: 3 dose levels (50mg, 150mg, 500mg bolus) crossed with 2 elimination rates (ke=0.05, ke=0.3). That's 6 combinations. Use SimBiology.Scenarios to set this up, simulate for 24 hours, and report Drug at t=24 for each combination as a table with Dose and ke as row labels. I want to verify that 500mg/ke=0.05 gives the highest remaining drug and 50mg/ke=0.3 gives the lowest.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches Scenarios API issues
All 6 combinations correctly labeled — result ordering not confused
assertions.expected: 500mg/ke=0.05 has highest Drug(t=24), 50mg/ke=0.3 has lowest, no label swaps

assertions.description: Agent correctly maps results to conditions — labels are consistent with physical expectations
assertions.expected: Results labeled correctly: within each ke, higher dose gives more Drug; within each dose, higher ke gives less Drug. No physically impossible label assignments.

assertions.description: Agent does not combine Scenarios with + operator (Rule 4)
assertions.expected: sc +

evals.name: Repeat dosing with correct simulation window
evals.description: Tests whether the agent sets StopTime long enough to capture all repeat doses. Without the skill, agents commonly set StopTime to 24 or 48 hours, silently truncating most doses with no error.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Simulate 200mg oral doses every 12 hours for 20 doses. Show me the Drug amount at trough (just before each dose) for all 20 doses. I want to see if the drug accumulates to a steady level.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
Trough values reported for all 20 doses — StopTime not truncated
assertions.expected: At least 18-20 trough values shown (not just 1-2 from a truncated simulation)

assertions.description: Troughs approach a steady-state level — later troughs stabilize
assertions.expected: Later trough values converge to a steady level (last 5 troughs within 5% of each other)

evals.name: Simulate model with unit conversion enabled
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly sets TimeUnits when enabling unit conversion — without it, simulation runs in seconds instead of the intended time unit, producing wildly wrong results.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a two-compartment PK model with units: Central compartment (10 liters, Drug 100 milligram), Peripheral compartment (20 liters, DrugP 0 milligram). Parameters: ke = 0.1/hour, k12 = 0.3/hour, k21 = 0.15/hour. All reactions are MassAction. Enable unit conversion and simulate for 24 hours. Show me Drug concentration (mg/L) in Central at t=0, t=12, and t=24.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches missing TimeUnits setting and unit mismatch issues
Drug concentration at t=24 shows significant decay from initial value — proves simulation ran in hours not seconds
assertions.expected: Drug concentration at t=24 is well below 5 mg/L (initial is 10 mg/L); values near 10 indicate time unit mismatch

evals.name: Virtual population simulation via Scenarios distributions
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses SimBiology.Scenarios with probability distribution objects for virtual population sampling, rather than manually generating parameter matrices.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a one-compartment PK model (Central, Drug=100, ke=0.1, elimination MassAction). I want to simulate a virtual population of 50 patients where ke varies according to a lognormal distribution (mean 0.1, CV 30%). Use SimBiology.Scenarios to set this up. Show me the mean and standard deviation of Drug at t=24 across the 50 patients.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect Scenarios distribution API usage
Mean and std of Drug at t=24 reported from 50 runs; std > 0 confirming variability
assertions.expected: Mean Drug(t=24) approximately 9-12, std > 0

assertions.description: Agent uses SimBiology.Scenarios with a distribution object rather than manually sampling and using createSimFunction with a parameter matrix
assertions.expected: add(sc, 'elementwise', ..., probDistObj, 'Number', N) pattern used

evals.name: Parameter sweep with dosing via SimFunction
evals.description: Tests whether the agent can pass doses to a SimFunction correctly using a dose table — passing a sbiodose object errors with 'Dosing information must be a table or cell array of tables'.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a one-compartment oral PK model (Depot -> Drug, elimination Drug -> null, ka=1.0, ke=0.1, MassAction). I want to compare three different dose amounts (100mg, 250mg, 500mg) given orally at time 0, across two elimination rates (ke=0.05, ke=0.2). Use createSimFunction with dosing support to run all 6 combinations efficiently. Report Drug at t=24 for each.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches passing sbiodose objects to SimFunction (must use dose tables)
All 6 combinations produce results with correct dose-response relationship
assertions.expected: Higher dose gives more Drug at t=24; higher ke gives less Drug at t=24
